projectDescription: A personal side project you've been working on for six months.

questions.snippet: Tasklight is a personal CLI for task lists.
questions.feedbackCorrect: This describes what the project is — its identity. That knowledge has a long shelf life.
questions.feedbackIncorrect: This isn't about what changed recently or why a choice was made. It answers: what is this project?

questions.snippet: Why SQLite: single file, no server, good for a solo side project.
questions.feedbackCorrect: This explains why a choice was made — it constrains future work.
questions.feedbackIncorrect: This isn't what the project is or what you're building next. It records rationale for a choice already made.

questions.snippet: Export should write JSON to stdout.
questions.feedbackCorrect: This defines intended behavior for a feature — what you're building.
questions.feedbackIncorrect: This isn't about what changed yesterday or why SQLite was chosen. It describes what the export feature should do.

questions.feedbackCorrect: This is a working rule for day-to-day execution. It can stay in your AI context file — it doesn't need its own contract.
questions.feedbackIncorrect: Not everything becomes a durable contract. Operating instructions can live in a trimmed CLAUDE.md without duplicating Entry or Handoff.

questions.snippet: Yesterday we fixed the SQLite locking bug in storage.py.
questions.feedbackCorrect: This is recent delta — what changed since the last session. It helps you continue instead of reconstruct.
questions.feedbackIncorrect: This isn't a permanent decision or project definition. It's what happened recently.

questions.snippet: We're planning an export command next.
questions.feedbackCorrect: This is current focus — what's active now. Session knowledge helps the next contributor pick up where you left off.
questions.feedbackIncorrect: Planning what's next is session continuity, not the full specification of how export should behave.

snippets: Tasklight is a personal CLI for task lists.
Why SQLite: single file, no server, good for a solo side project.
Export should write JSON to stdout.
Yesterday we fixed the SQLite locking bug in storage.py.
We're planning an export command next.
